channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IEBC Reports Over 1.8 Million New Voter Registrations Across Kenya
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) in Kenya has welcomed over 1.8 million citizens who registered during the 30-day Enhanced Continuous Voter Registration (ECVR) exercise. IEBC...

item.title: Global Energy Crisis Renews Interest in Green Hydrogen
item.description: The article explains how the ongoing global energy crisis is pushing governments and energy planners to look again at green hydrogen as part of a broader energy security strategy. With fossil fuel pri...

item.title: Tanzania Commission Urges Fair Legal Accountability for Election Violence
item.description: The Chairman of Tanzania's Commission of Inquiry into the violence that happened during and after the October 29, 2025 general election, Judge Mohamed Chande Othman, emphasized the need for accountabi...

item.title: Critical mineral mining for clean energy and AI is straining water resources and harming vulnerable communities
item.description: The global push toward clean energy, digital technologies, and artificial intelligence relies heavily on critical minerals such as lithium, cobalt, copper, and rare earth elements. While these materia...

item.title: Winnipeg’s Giordano Vaccaro Selected First Overall in 2026 CFL Draft by Ottawa Redblacks
item.description: Giordano Vaccaro, a 22-year-old offensive lineman from Winnipeg, has been chosen as the first overall pick in the 2026 CFL draft by the Ottawa Redblacks. Standing six feet two inches tall and weighing...

item.title: Kenya Welcomes Four Rare Mountain Bongo Males to Support Conservation Efforts
item.description: Kenya has taken a significant step in conserving one of the world’s most endangered antelopes, the mountain bongo, with the arrival of four rare male bongos from the Czech Republic. The animals were c...

item.title: Analysing the Implications of Palantir’s Technological Manifesto
item.description: Earlier this month, Palantir, a multibillion-dollar US tech company, shared on X a summary of CEO Alex Karp’s book, 'The Technological Republic: Hard Power, Soft Belief, and the Future of the West'. T...

item.title: How Personal Traits and Experiences Shape Your Dreams
item.description: Recent research from the IMT School for Advanced Studies Lucca reveals that dreams are far from random. Analyzing over 3,700 dream and waking experience reports from 287 participants, the study found ...

item.title: The growing role of art therapy in mental health support
item.description: In recent years, the landscape of mental health treatment in India has witnessed a significant transformation with the growing prominence of art therapy. Unlike traditional talk-based counselling, whi...

item.title: Chery to locally produce Jetour vehicles and hybrid bakkies at Rosslyn plant
item.description: Chinese automaker Chery has confirmed plans to produce either the Jetour T1 or T2 model at its Rosslyn plant in Pretoria, which it recently acquired from Nissan. In addition to these SUVs, Chery is ex...

item.title: Pakistan’s Role as a Mediator in US-Iran Conflict and Its Domestic Challenges
item.description: Pakistan has recently gained international attention for acting as a mediator in the US-Iran conflict, which has led many to describe it as a middle power. The country’s diplomatic efforts are being a...

item.title: Britain’s Strategic Challenge: Safeguarding AI Sovereignty Amid Global Technological Dominance
item.description: The article highlights the increasing strategic and technological dependence of Britain on US tech companies, emphasising AI as a critical domain for future economic, scientific, and military advantag...

item.title: Authorities continue search for missing five-year-old Sharon Granites in Alice Springs
item.description: The search for five-year-old Sharon Granites in Alice Springs has entered its fourth day, with Northern Territory police expressing confidence that the girl is still alive. Sharon was last seen at her...

item.title: Iran Negotiations Stall Amid U.S. Efforts to Exit Conflict
item.description: Negotiations between the U.S. and Iran remain stalled, with little progress toward a resolution of the ongoing conflict. President Donald Trump has canceled planned visits by JD Vance, Steve Witkoff, ...

item.title: Samsung Launches Galaxy S26 Ultra with AI Features for Professional Productivity
item.description: Samsung has unveiled the Galaxy S26 Ultra, its first smartphone designed with agentic AI to enhance productivity for Canadian professionals. The device acts as a personal operations coordinator, integ...

item.title: Congress to Investigate April 19 Armed Encounter in Negros Occidental
item.description: Lawmakers in the Philippine House of Representatives are seeking an official inquiry into the armed encounter that occurred on April 19 in Barangay Salamanca, Municipality of Toboso, Negros Occidental...

item.title: Practical Tips for Improving Your AI Prompting Skills
item.description: In this article, Jim VandeHei from Axios provides practical guidance for getting the most out of AI tools like ChatGPT and Claude. He emphasizes that the difference between average and high-value AI u...

item.title: Building Water and Coastal Resilience in South Africa
item.description: South Africa faces a significant challenge with water usage, as between 30 and 60 percent of treated urban water is used solely for flushing toilets. This is compounded by leaks, which can waste tens ...

item.title: Woman reports street harassment and untaxed vehicle after being targeted by catcallers
item.description: Aneesa, a 25-year-old teacher in Cardiff, experienced harassment when two men in a van repeatedly catcalled and honked at her and her younger sister while crossing Newport Road. Initially mistaking th...

item.title: PNP Declares Full Alert for Labor Day Protests Nationwide
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) will implement a heightened alert starting at 12:01 a.m. on April 30 until 12:01 a.m. on May 2 in preparation for Labor Day activities and protests across the coun...
